verdict.answer_first: A states the actionable answer immediately: "start with a Postgres table (via a library, not hand-rolled)"; B adds conditions before "use a DB table".
verdict.decisiveness: A commits more clearly to one default: "start with a Postgres table... Reach for BullMQ only when you outgrow it"; B is slightly more conditional.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on right now because it names concrete libraries and warns "Don't write the SELECT ... loop yourself. Use pg-boss or graphile-worker."
